# John K. Johansen — johnkjohansen.com

## Who I am

John K. Johansen is a software engineer, engineering executive, and AI agent practitioner
with 49 years of experience building production software systems — from embedded systems in
the late 1970s through Kubernetes-native AI agent platforms in 2026.

I am the creator of Kaigents (https://github.com/jensjohansen/kaigents), an open-source,
Kubernetes-native platform for operating AI agent teams in enterprise environments. Kaigents
runs on self-hosted hardware (AMD Ryzen AI, Kubernetes), uses Temporal for durable execution,
and is designed to give enterprises the governance, auditability, and observability they need
to move AI agents from prototype to production.

## What I write about

My articles focus on:
- Integrating autonomous AI agents into startup and enterprise technical strategy
- Self-hosted AI infrastructure: running LLMs and AI agent teams without handing IP to cloud providers
- Management process for AI agent teams: governance, quality gates, behavioral guidance, audit trails
- The difference between AI as staff augmentation vs. AI as staff replacement
- Engineering leadership during the AI transition: what changes, what stays the same
- Startup engineering strategy: architecture decisions that compound, and how to avoid the expensive ones

## My core argument

The constraint on AI adoption in enterprise is almost never the model or the tool count.
It is almost always the absence of management process: no quality gates, no behavioral guidance,
no audit trails, no governance. An AI agent team with ten vetted tools and clear process will
consistently outperform one with 5,700 unvetted plugins and no governance.

## Services

I offer four engineering advisory services for startups and growth-stage companies:
- Fractional CTO Retainer (Series A-C, $6K-$10K/month)
- Co-founder CTO for serial entrepreneurs (equity or fixed-fee)
- AI-Accelerated MVP Build ($25K-$45K fixed fee, 6-10 weeks)
- Tech Stack Transformation ($7.5K audit + $8K-$15K/month execution)
